feed.title: Helena Richie
feed.subtitle: I'm a Physics PhD candidate at the University of Pittsburgh working with Evan Schneider on creating high resolution galaxy simulations. I'm interested in how dust affects galaxy evolution, specifically how it evolves and moves throughout galaxies.

entry.title: New Dust in Galaxy Outflow Simulations!

link.title: New Dust in Galaxy Outflow Simulations!

entry.summary: High-redshift analogue Evolution of 1, 0.1, 0.01, and 0.001 micron radius dust grains (from left to right) under sputtering in a multi-phase galactic outflow. The galaxy has a star formation rate of 20 solar masses per year, with clusters distribuetd throughout the disk at a scale radius of 800 pc. M82 analogue Evolution of 1, 0.1, 0.01, and 0.001 micron radius dust grains (from left to ...

entry.title: Dust Survival in Galactic Winds
entry.summary: This post is a summary of Richie et al. 2024, my first paper on dust evolution in galactic outflows. In this paper, we used idealized simulations of individual dusty clouds in hot galactic winds to understand how sputtering affects dust in these environments. We ran a suite of simulations to explore how different factors, such as cloud evolution and dust grain size, affect dust survival. In the...

entry.title: Introduction
entry.summary: Welcome to my website! Here you can find some general information about my research and movies of my simulations.
